No results. Let me proceed from local evidence and biophysics. Key insight from the ThT amyloid literature: inhibitors act in two regimes — kinetic (delay, same plateau) and thermodynamic/stoichiometric (lower plateau by sequestering monomer into off-pathway or reducing reachable fibril mass). The current parent uses denominator-only (rate slowing, fixed plateau). The breakthrough idea moves the plateau.

I'll implement effective-capacity inhibition. But rather than dropping the useful autocatalytic secondary channel entirely, I'll keep the structure minimal per the idea (6 constants) while ensuring numerical safety. The plateau shrinks with inhibitor and grows with monomer. Since x1 (monomer) is ~1e-6, I need the plateau term structured so constants are well-conditioned — but the least-squares fitter handles scaling, as seen in parent. The additive c0 keeps plateau near normalized range even when monomer term is tiny.

Let me implement it.
